rationale: Scope fence + arity checks, all done from an /tmp git-archive copy of 33a8ce8d (shared worktree untouched, git status clean throughout). `git diff 6f6634b4 33a8ce8d --stat` = exactly the 6 stated internal/complete/* files, +374/-881; no internal/panel or internal/bead byte change this round (0-line diff both against 6f6634b4 and, for internal/panel, against ce9f5395 — O2's anti-gaming claim holds too). No ADR/plan/doc/skill/cmd-verb files touched. Full-bead scope (1906e39e..33a8ce8d --name-only) stays within internal/bead|complete|panel; panel_gate_e2e_test.go/panel_gate_layout_test.go changed mid-bead but are byte-identical to 1906e39e, so they're correctly absent from that name-only list. `panelGate(` has exactly one definition, `(beadID string, roots []string, wtPath string, panelGateEnabled bool, warnOut io.Writer) (*panel.Registration, error)`, and every one of the 13 call sites (1 prod in complete.go:387, 12 in three test files) destructures exactly 2 return values -- uniform 2-tuple, no 3-tuple/[]panel.Refutation leftovers. reconcilePendingRefutations is `(beadID string) error` with zero panel.Scan/ForBead/Tally calls in its body; grep for dischargeVerified/dischargeEvidence/dischargedEntry/decodeDischargedEntries/unionDischargedEntries/writeRefutationDischargedMetadata/dedupeSortedSlugs/refutation_discharged/appliedOrigins/panelTallyFn(in reconcile) is clean (panelTallyFn's one remaining use is the unrelated, pre-existing panelAdvisory path). `go build ./...`, gofmt, go vet, targeted panel/complete/bead tests, full `go test ./internal/panel ./internal/complete ./internal/bead ./cmd/mindspec`, and `-race` on the three packages all green. Mutation-probed the reconcile write-error swallow in the /tmp copy: TestPanelRefuted_SatisfyWriteFailure_FailsCompletion goes RED when the write-error is swallowed, PASS when restored -- confirms it's load-bearing, not hollow.

findings.note: Confirmed via git diff --stat and --name-only against 6f6634b4/1906e39e: 6 internal/complete files only this round; full bead stays within internal/panel|complete|bead; no ADR/plan/doc/skill/CLI edits.
panelGate is a uniform 2-tuple (*panel.Registration, error) at its 1 definition and all 13 call sites (grep -rn 'panelGate(').
grep for the enumerated deleted discharge/origin symbols across internal/cmd returns nothing live; reconcilePendingRefutations makes zero panel reads.
